source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
An opamp has a finite open-loop voltage gain of 100. Its input offset voltage \( V_{\text{ios}} \) (equal to \( +5 \text{mV} \)) is modeled as shown in <image>. The amplifier is ideal in all other respects. \( V_{\text{input}} \) is 25 mV.

The output voltage (in millivolts) is ________

the circuit topology and offset-source placement/polarity are not fully specified in text alone
multi_step: solving requires combining finite open-loop gain, input offset voltage, and the given input voltage
the figure-based op-amp model likely requires forming at least one circuit relation before computing output
answer is numeric and also appears in 4 multiple-choice options, suggesting a derived calculation rather than direct lookup
verifiability: a single exact answer_text is provided: 413.79
multiple-choice options are provided, enabling straightforward final-answer checking
image asset exists with recorded dimensions and format

agent_assessment.rationale: The text clearly states the op-amp parameters and asks for the output voltage, and the figure clearly provides the required circuit topology: a non-inverting input with stacked Vios and Vinput sources, and a feedback network of 15 kΩ and 1 kΩ at the inverting input. The image is necessary and semantically readable enough to determine the intended circuit.
